heb.paragraph: "Show me your ways (dərāk̅ek̅ā), LORD, teach me your paths" (v.4). An alphabetic acrostic prayer. David asks for guidance, forgiveness, and instruction. Key themes: trust (v.2), teaching (vv.4–5), mercy (v.6), forgiveness (v.7), humility (v.9).

panels.ctx: The first acrostic psalm. Each verse begins with a successive letter of the Hebrew alphabet. David asks God to lead, teach, guide, and forgive. The mood is humble and teachable.

cross.note: "Trust in the LORD with all your heart... he will make your paths straight" — the wisdom parallel.

notes.note: "Show me your ways, teach me your paths, guide me in your truth." Three requests: show, teach, guide. David wants direction, not just information.
"Remember your mercy and love, for they are from of old. Do not remember the sins of my youth." Mercy remembered; sins forgotten. David asks God for selective memory.

alter.source: Robert Alter, The Hebrew Bible: A Translation with Commentary — Scholarly Paraphrase

net.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

sections.header: Verses 12–22 — “The LORD Confides in Those Who Fear Him”

heb.paragraph: "The LORD confides (sōd) in those who fear him" (v.14). Sōd is intimate counsel — the inner circle’s conversation. God shares his secrets with the reverent. Fear of the LORD produces not distance but intimacy.

panels.ctx: God guides the humble (v.9), confides in the God-fearing (v.14), frees from snares (v.15). David’s eyes are always on the LORD (v.15). The psalm closes with a plea for Israel’s redemption (v.22).

cross.note: "I no longer call you servants... I have called you friends" — the NT fulfilment of God’s intimate counsel.
"He takes the upright into his confidence" — the same sōd theology.

notes.note: "The LORD confides in those who fear him." The paradox: fear produces intimacy. Reverence opens the door to friendship.
"My eyes are ever on the LORD." Sustained attention. David’s gaze is fixed. The direction of the eyes determines the direction of the life.

alter.source: Robert Alter, The Hebrew Bible: A Translation with Commentary — Scholarly Paraphrase

net.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

ppl.text: David asks God to lead, teach, guide, and forgive…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVDo not remember the sins of my youth and my rebellious ways; according to your love remember me, for you, Lord, are good.ESVRemember not the sins of my youth or my transgressions; according to your steadfast love remember me, for the sake of your goodness, O LORD!NIVRemember, Lord, your great mercy and love, for they are from of old.ESVRemember your mercy, O LORD, and your steadfast love, for they have been from of old.

rec.text: ""Show me your ways, teach me your paths, guide me in your truth." Three requests: show, teach, guide. David wants direction, not just information."

rows.label: The Intimacy — 25:12–22
rows.text: "The LORD confides in those who fear him." Intimacy through reverence. Eyes always on God.

"The LORD confides (sōd) in those who fear him" (v.14). Sōd is intimate counsel — the inner circle’s conversation. God shares his secrets with the reverent. Fear of the LORD produces not distance but intimacy.

themes.note: Mercy: the plea for forgiveness and guidance. Faith: eyes always on the LORD. Covenant: God’s intimate counsel with the faithful.
